entities.name: HETERODERA CAJANI
entities.description: A species of cyst nematode that parasitizes pigeonpea roots.

entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: The host legume plant, specifically cultivar ICPL 87, used in the study.

entities.name: SOIL MOISTURE
entities.description: The level of water in the soil, tested at 16%, 24%, 32%, and 40%.

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The thermal environment, tested at 20 C, 25 C, and fluctuating between 25 and 32 C.

entities.name: PENETRATION
entities.description: The ability of second-stage nematode juveniles to enter the roots of the host plant.

entities.name: DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The growth and maturation process of the nematode within the host plant.

entities.name: REPRODUCTION
entities.description: The ability of the nematode to produce offspring, measured by the number of females per root system.

entities.name: PLANT GROWTH
entities.description: The physical development and growth of the pigeonpea plant.

entities.name: REPRODUCTIVE FACTOR
entities.description: A numerical value used to quantify the reproductive success of the nematode.

entities.name: GROWTH CHAMBERS
entities.description: Controlled environmental systems used to maintain specific temperature and moisture levels.

entities.name: GREENHOUSE
entities.description: A controlled environment where temperature fluctuated between 25 and 32 C.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The geographical region where pigeonpea is grown and H. cajani densities are predicted.
